products.name: Current Transformers
products.shortDescription: Current transformers up to 2500/5A ratio with accuracy class 0.5 & 1.0 and burden up to 30VA for metering and protection applications.
products.description: Alliance Engineering Current Transformers are designed for accurate current measurement, metering, and protection in LT and HT electrical systems. Available in ratios up to 2500/5A with Class 0.5 and Class 1.0 accuracy and burden ratings up to 30VA. Suitable for panel boards, switchgear, substations, DG panels, APFC panels, and energy monitoring systems. Manufactured under ISO 9001:2015 standards and compliant with IS 2705 and IEC 61869-2.
products.applicationNote: Suitable for indoor LT and HT panel applications requiring accurate current measurement and protection relay interfacing. Designed for long operational life and stable accuracy.

details: Standard ratios from 5/5A to 2500/5A
Custom ratios available on request
Dual ratio CTs available
Suitable for LT and HT systems
Class 0.5 for precision metering applications
Class 1.0 for general measurement applications
Stable accuracy over operating range
Suitable for utility and industrial installations
2.5VA for compact metering applications
5VA and 10VA for standard panels
15VA and 30VA for relay and protection systems
Custom burden ratings available
Ring type CTs for cable passing
Bar primary CTs for busbar mounting
Compact panel mounting design
Easy installation and maintenance

routineTests: Ratio test
Polarity verification
Insulation resistance test
High voltage withstand test
Burden and accuracy verification
Visual and dimensional inspection

ordering.paymentTerms: Advance / As per PO for established customers

faqs.question: What maximum ratio is available?
faqs.answer: Current transformers are available up to 2500/5A ratio in standard models. Custom ratios can also be manufactured on request.

faqs.question: Which accuracy classes are available?
faqs.answer: We offer Class 0.5 and Class 1.0 current transformers suitable for metering and industrial applications.

faqs.question: What burden ratings are supported?
faqs.answer: Standard burden ratings include 2.5VA, 5VA, 10VA, 15VA, and 30VA depending on application requirements.

faqs.question: Are these suitable for protection relays?
faqs.answer: Yes. Higher burden CTs are suitable for relay and protection applications in switchgear and substations.

faqs.question: Do you provide test certificates?
faqs.answer: Yes. All current transformers are supplied with routine test certificates after inspection and testing.

whyAllianceEngineering.detail: Designed for stable and reliable current measurement across industrial applications.
Ratios available up to 2500/5A with multiple burden options.
High-quality CRGO core and robust epoxy insulation for long operational life.
Manufactured as per IS 2705 and IEC 61869-2 standards.
Each unit undergoes routine testing before dispatch.

products.name: Control Transformers
products.shortDescription: Single and three-phase control transformers for industrial control panels, CNC machines, cranes, switchgear, and railway applications. Up to 30 KVA.
products.description: Alliance Engineering Control Transformers are dry-type, isolation transformers engineered to supply stable low-voltage power to industrial control circuits under high inrush conditions. Designed for relay panels, PLCs, motor starters, CNC machines, cranes, switchgear, and railway control cubicles. Available from 25 VA to 30 KVA in single and three-phase configurations, with Class B, F, and H insulation. 100% copper wound on CRGO core. Routine-tested before dispatch with test certificate. ISO 9001:2015 manufactured. Compliant with IS 11171 and IEC 61558.

technicalSpecifications.specification: Class B (130°C) / Class F (155°C) / Class H (180°C)
2 kV AC for 1 minute (winding to core/earth)

products.applicationNote: Verified for industrial control panel, CNC machine tool, crane, switchgear, railway, and substation applications. Designed for high inrush conditions (6–10× rated current) typical of relay and contactor energisation.

customizations.description: 25 VA / 50 VA / 100 VA / 250 VA / 500 VA / 1 KVA / 2 KVA / 5 KVA / 10 KVA / 15 KVA / 20 KVA / 25 KVA / 30 KVA (custom ratings available)
details: Smaller VA ratings (25–250 VA) for relay coil and pilot circuit supply
Mid-range (500 VA–5 KVA) for PLC panels and motor starters
Higher KVA (10–30 KVA) for large switchgear and multi-feeder panels
Custom intermediate KVA ratings engineered on request
High inrush capacity factor (200–1000%) available for heavy contactor loads
Single Phase: standard for most control circuits with 110V or 230V secondary
Three Phase: 415V primary, for large panels or multi-secondary configurations
Auto-transformer variant available for ±10% step-up/step-down applications
Standard secondary: 110V AC (most common for relay/contactor circuits)
24V AC secondary for PLC and safety relay circuits
Dual secondary winding: e.g. 415V primary → 230V + 110V simultaneously
Custom voltage ratios engineered on request
Voltage tappings: ±2.5% and ±5% of primary available
Copper: lower DC resistance, better conductivity, smaller size, longer life — preferred for all applications
Aluminium: lighter weight, lower cost — suitable for budget-sensitive builds
Both options fully specified and documented at order stage

customizations.description: Class B (130°C) / Class F (155°C) / Class H (180°C)
details: Class B: standard use, ambient temperature ≤40°C, well-ventilated panels
Class F: elevated ambient, VFD panels, steel plant environments
Class H: high-duty continuous, tropical / desert environments, railway applications
VPI (Vacuum Pressure Impregnation) treatment standard on Class F and H

customizations.description: Open Type (IP00) / Sheet Metal Enclosed (IP21–IP54) / Resin Cast (IP64) / Oil-Cooled

Sheet metal enclosed (IP21–IP54): standalone installation, powder-coated RAL 7035
Resin cast / epoxy moulded: moisture-proof, IP64, outdoor-capable, coastal environments
Oil-cooled: for higher KVA ratings requiring enhanced heat dissipation
Stainless steel enclosure available for corrosive / chemical environments
Fixed ratio 2-winding: standard isolation control transformer
Auto-transformer: single winding, step-up/down, cost-effective for ±10% voltage correction
Variable (Variac): 0–270V output, hand-wheel or motorised control
Motorised Variac: remote control via 0–10V or 4–20 mA signal input

customizations.description: None (standard) / Primary HRC Fuse / Primary MCB / Primary MCB + Secondary Fuse + Thermal Relay
details: Factory-fitted primary HRC fuse for short-circuit protection
MCB on primary for overload and short-circuit protection with manual reset
Full protection package: primary MCB + secondary HRC fuse + thermal overload relay
Railway-grade: primary and secondary fuse links as per RDSO specification
RDSO-specification build: enhanced dielectric strength, vibration-proof terminals, stainless hardware
Custom nameplate / OEM branding for panel builder supply
Reduced-regulation design for voltage-sensitive loads
High inrush capacity factor (HIC) design for heavy contactor panels

routineTests: Turns ratio measurement (primary to secondary) — digital turns ratio meter
No-load loss and no-load current at rated voltage and frequency
Load loss (copper loss) at rated current
Insulation resistance — 500V DC megger (minimum 100 MΩ)
Dielectric (Hi-Pot) test — 2 kV AC for 1 minute, winding to core/earth
Winding resistance measurement (DC) — primary and secondary
Polarity and phase relationship check
Visual and dimensional inspection per approved drawing
typeTests: Temperature rise test at rated load — insulation class compliance
Short-circuit withstand test — mechanical and thermal
Induced overvoltage test at 2× rated voltage for 60 seconds
Noise level measurement — dB(A) at 1 m
Vibration and shock test (railway-grade units, per RDSO spec)

ordering.example: CT-TP-100-415-110-F-54 = Three Phase, 10 KVA, 415V/110V, Class F, IP54

ordering.paymentTerms: Advance / As per PO for established customers

faqs.question: What is the difference between a control transformer and a distribution transformer?
faqs.answer: A distribution transformer is designed for continuous sinusoidal load currents. A control transformer is engineered for the high inrush currents (6–10× rated) produced when relay coils, solenoids, and contactors energise simultaneously. Control transformers have a higher inrush capacity factor (200–1000%) to ensure the secondary voltage does not collapse during these transients.

faqs.question: Can you supply transformers with dual or multiple secondary windings?
faqs.answer: Yes. Multi-winding designs with two or more secondary voltages (e.g. 415V primary → 230V + 110V simultaneously) are a standard option. Please specify required voltages and KVA for each winding in your enquiry.

faqs.question: What insulation class should I choose — Class B or Class F?
faqs.answer: Class B (130°C max) is suitable for most standard control panel applications with good ventilation. Class F (155°C max) is recommended for high ambient temperature environments (steel plants, foundries), duty-cycle applications, or where higher reliability margins are required. Class H (180°C) is available for extreme conditions and railway applications.

faqs.question: Do you offer RDSO-approved transformers for railways?
faqs.answer: RDSO vendor approval is in progress. We currently supply railway-grade control transformers built to RDSO specification standards to railway contractors and system integrators.

faqs.question: What is the standard warranty?
faqs.answer: Alliance Engineering provides a 12-month warranty from date of dispatch against manufacturing defects in materials and workmanship. Extended warranty up to 3 years is available on request.

faqs.question: Do you provide test reports with each unit?
faqs.answer: Yes. A routine test report (turns ratio, insulation resistance, Hi-Pot, winding resistance, no-load loss) is issued with every unit at no extra cost. Type test reports for standard designs are available on request.

faqs.question: What is the minimum order quantity?
faqs.answer: There is no minimum order quantity. Single-unit orders are accepted. Volume discounts apply for orders of 5 units and above.

faqs.question: Can I order through GeM or IREPS?
faqs.answer: Yes. Alliance Engineering is a registered GeM seller. Search 'Control Transformer' on gem.gov.in and filter by our seller ID. For IREPS orders, contact us with the applicable drawing or specification to attach with your RFQ.

whyAllianceEngineering.detail: Every transformer is produced under a documented quality management system — from incoming material inspection to final dispatch.
No substitution with aluminium windings or low-grade core steel. Lower losses, better regulation, and longer service life — standard.
Non-standard voltages, special KVA ratings, dual secondaries, railway specifications — engineered to your exact requirement.
Transformers supplied to POWERGRID, HVPN, JSW Steel, and Indian Railways installations across the region.
Every unit routine-tested before dispatch. Test certificates issued as standard.
Government and PSU buyers can procure directly through GeM and IREPS portals with pre-verified documentation.

products.name: Industrial Battery Chargers
products.shortDescription: Industrial battery chargers up to 30A for substations, telecom, solar, UPS, and genset panel applications.
products.description: Alliance Engineering Industrial Battery Chargers are designed for reliable, continuous duty charging of lead-acid and VRLA batteries in substations, telecom shelters, UPS systems, solar installations, and genset panels. Available in Float, Boost, and CCCV (Constant Current Constant Voltage) charging modes with full automatic switching. Output voltage: 12V / 24V / 48V / 110V DC. Charging current up to 30A. Built-in protections: OVP, UVP, OCP, reverse polarity, and over-temperature. ISO 9001:2015 manufactured. Compliant with IS 8320 and IEC 60896.

faqs.question: What is the maximum charging current available?
faqs.answer: The maximum charging current in our standard range is 30A, suitable for battery banks up to 300 Ah. Current is adjustable from 5A to 30A via the front-panel potentiometer. For applications requiring higher currents, please contact us for a custom solution.

faqs.question: What battery types are compatible?
faqs.answer: Our chargers are compatible with flooded lead-acid, VRLA, AGM, and gel batteries. The charge profile (float voltage, boost voltage, equalization) is factory-set per battery type and is also field-adjustable. Specify your battery type when ordering.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Float, Boost, and CCCV modes?
faqs.answer: Float mode maintains a fully charged battery at a low trickle voltage (13.5–13.8V for a 12V system) to prevent self-discharge. Boost mode applies a higher voltage (14.4–14.7V) to rapidly recharge a discharged battery. CCCV (Constant Current Constant Voltage) is a two-stage automatic mode — bulk charge at constant current until near full, then switch to constant voltage float. CCCV is the recommended mode for maximising battery life.

faqs.question: Can the charger run continuously in unattended locations?
faqs.answer: Yes. The automatic SMPS and Float-Boost CCCV models are designed for continuous unattended float duty — typical in substations and telecom shelters. The charger automatically maintains the battery once fully charged with no manual intervention required.

faqs.question: What protections are built in?
faqs.answer: Standard protections include Over-Voltage (OVP), Under-Voltage (UVP), Over-Current (OCP), Short-Circuit, Reverse Polarity, and Over-Temperature (OTP) with auto-recovery. All faults trigger a front-panel LED alarm. Dry contact alarm output for SCADA/BMS integration is available on request.

faqs.question: Is a test certificate provided?
faqs.answer: Yes. Every unit is tested before dispatch — output voltage accuracy, current limiting, OVP/OCP trip verification, ripple measurement, and Hi-Pot test. A test report is issued with each unit at no extra charge.

faqs.question: Can I order through GeM?
faqs.answer: Yes. Alliance Engineering is a registered GeM seller. Search 'Industrial Battery Charger' on gem.gov.in and filter by our seller ID. IREPS orders are also accepted — contact us with your specification document.

whyAllianceEngineering.detail: Every unit manufactured and tested under a documented quality management system.
Designed for 24×7 unattended operation in substations and telecom shelters — not just occasional charging.
OVP, OCP, OTP, reverse polarity, and short-circuit protection built in as standard — no add-ons needed.
Configurable charge profiles for flooded, VRLA, AGM, and gel batteries — one charger platform, multiple applications.
Supplied to substation and telecom installations including POWERGRID, HVPN, and railway contractors.
Government and PSU buyers can procure directly through GeM and IREPS portals.

3Ph 4W: unbalanced loads with neutral — most industrial panels use this

customizations.description: No Comm / RS485 Modbus RTU (digital models only)
details: Modbus RTU: read V/A/Hz/PF remotely from PLC/SCADA
Baud rate: 1200–9600 bps
Address: user-programmable 1–247
Requires 2-wire RS485 + GND

seo.metaTitle: Digital & Analog Meters | Alliance Engineering Company
seo.metaDescription: Panel-mount digital and analog meters for voltage, current, frequency. IS 1248, IEC 60051 compliant.

products.name: DC Shunts
products.shortDescription: Manganin alloy DC shunts for high-accuracy current measurement in industrial applications.
products.description: Manganin alloy DC Shunts for Electroplating, Welding, DC Drives, Battery, and Solar applications. Current range: 10A–10,000A, mV Output: 50/75/100 mV.
products.applicationNote: Verified for high-current DC measurement in industrial and solar installations.

details: Lower ratings (10–50A): small PCB/panel shunts
100–500A: standard industrial
1000A+: busbar-mounted, large copper sections
All ratings for 50/75/100 mV output

customizations.description: 50 mV / 75 mV / 100 mV at rated current
details: 50 mV: lowest power loss, preferred for high-current
75 mV: DIN standard, most common
100 mV: highest signal, better for cheap meters
All compatible with ALECS digital meters
Class 0.2: precision lab/test use
Class 0.5: revenue metering, solar
Class 1.0: general industrial monitoring
Higher accuracy = tighter alloy tolerance, higher cost

customizations.description: Panel / Busbar Direct / Insulated Base / External (floor-standing for >2000A)
details: Panel mount: enclosed, terminals inside panel
Busbar direct: bolt-on for busbars >500A
Insulated base: 1000V isolation from chassis
Floor-standing: for 5000A+ with forced cooling

seo.metaDescription: Manganin alloy DC Shunts 10A–10,000A with 50/75/100 mV output. IEC 60051, DIN 43703 compliant.

products.name: Dimmers (Variable Transformers / Variacs) - abcxyz
products.shortDescription: Single and three-phase variable transformers (variacs) for lab, electroplating, and heating applications.
products.description: Variable Transformers (Variacs/Dimmers) for Lab, Electroplating, Heating, and Motor Testing applications. Output: 0–270V AC, Rating: 0.5–20 kVA.
products.applicationNote: Verified for laboratory, electroplating, and industrial heating applications.

details: 1–2A: bench lab use
5–10A: electroplating small tanks
20–50A: heating elements
100A: large industrial loads
Stack multiple units for higher ratings
1Ph (230V): most applications
3Ph (415V): balanced 3-phase loads, furnaces, 3-phase motor testing
3Ph unit = 3× single-phase stacked on common shaft

customizations.description: Open Coil / Enclosed Metal Box / Panel Mount
details: Open coil: lowest cost, IP00, bench use with guard
Enclosed metal box: IP21, dust protection
Panel mount: flush-mount knob with enclosed coil behind panel
Manual: direct hand-wheel or knob
Motorised: AC motor drive, remote 0–10V / 4–20 mA input, full 0–100% range control from PLC/SCADA
Motorised adds ~30% cost

seo.metaTitle: Dimmers (Variable Transformers / Variacs) | Alliance Engineering Company
seo.metaDescription: Variable Transformers (Variacs) 0–270V AC, 0.5–20 kVA for lab, electroplating, and heating. IS 5142, IEC 61558 compliant.

products.name: DC Power Pack
products.shortDescription: DC power packs for battery charging, substation auxiliary supply, and lab applications.
products.description: DC Power Pack for Battery Charging, Substation Auxiliary, Lab, and DC Control applications. Input: 220–240V AC, Output: adjustable/fixed DC, Current: 1–50A.
products.applicationNote: Verified for substation auxiliary, battery charging, and laboratory DC supply.

details: Fixed: plug-and-play for known loads
Adjustable: versatile, for testing or variable loads
Wide-range 0–240V option for maximum flexibility
1–5A: control relays, small solenoids
10–20A: battery float charging
30–50A: heavy-duty charging, motor field supply
Higher currents add forced-air cooling fan

customizations.description: Open Frame / Enclosed Metal Box / ALECS Branded Enclosure
details: Open: for panel integration (customer provides enclosure)
Enclosed: standalone unit, ventilated MS box
ALECS branded: with logo plate, standard grey finish, standard sizes

seo.metaDescription: DC Power Pack 1–50A for battery charging, substation auxiliary, and lab. IS 13947, IEC 60439, CE compliant.

products.name: Bus-Bar Insulators (ALECS Brand)
products.shortDescription: Epoxy resin and SMC/DMC busbar insulators for LV and MV switchgear and panels.
products.description: ALECS Brand Bus-Bar Insulators in Epoxy Resin, SMC, and DMC for Control Panels, Switchgear, Power Distribution, HV/LV applications. Voltage: 415V–11kV.
products.applicationNote: Verified for LV and MV panel busbar support and switchgear insulation.

customizations.description: Epoxy Resin / SMC (Sheet Moulding Compound) / DMC (Dough Moulding Compound)
details: Epoxy: highest strength, lowest tracking, best for HV
SMC: good mechanical, cost-effective for LV
DMC: complex shapes, intricate profiles, LV only
Specify for voltage class

customizations.description: Cap & Stud / Channel / Flat Cleat / Stand-off / Tank Clamp
details: Cap & Stud: most common, vertical busbar support
Channel/L-shape: horizontal busbar run
Flat Cleat: bolt-on, double busbar
Stand-off: elevated mount, air clearance
Tank Clamp: transformer-to-busbar connection
415V: standard panel
1.1 kV: LV industrial, motor control
3.3/6.6/11 kV: MV switchgear, must specify correct BIL
Creepage and clearance increase with voltage class

customizations.description: Red / Black / Blue / Grey / Custom RAL
details: Red: standard industry colour, L1/Phase
Black: N/neutral
Blue: DC+
Grey: enclosure blend
Colour helps phase identification — R/Y/B phase coding available

customizations.description: M6 / M8 / M10 / M12 / M16 and custom hole pattern
details: M6/M8: small panels, ≤100A busbars
M10/M12: standard LV panels 100–1000A
M16: heavy busbars 1000A+
Custom bolt circle patterns for OEM panel builders

seo.metaTitle: Bus-Bar Insulators (ALECS Brand) | Alliance Engineering Company
seo.metaDescription: ALECS Brand Epoxy, SMC, DMC busbar insulators for LV/MV panels 415V–11kV. IS 5516, IEC 60660 compliant.

products.name: LED Indicator (ALECS Brand)
products.shortDescription: ALECS Brand LED indicators for control panels, machine tools, and process equipment.
products.description: ALECS Brand LED Indicators for Control Panels, Machine Tools, and Process Equipment. Diameter: 8–30 mm, Voltage: 12–230V AC/DC, LED Life: >50,000h.
products.applicationNote: Verified for industrial panel indication and machine status monitoring.

details: 8mm: sub-panel, space-constrained
16mm: compact panel
22mm: most common, standard panel cutout
30mm: large operator panel, easy visibility from distance
Red: fault/alarm/stop
Green: run/OK/power on
Yellow/Amber: warning/standby
Blue: information
White: general purpose — always specify at order
Must exactly match control circuit voltage
DC models: observe polarity
230V AC: most common for panels
24V DC: PLC output standard
Never mix AC/DC voltages
Standard: visual only
With buzzer: audible + visual in one unit, 85 dB piezo
Saves panel space and wiring
Buzzer acknowledges via panel LED ack contact

seo.metaTitle: LED Indicator (ALECS Brand) | Alliance Engineering Company
seo.metaDescription: ALECS Brand LED indicators 8–30mm, 12–230V AC/DC, >50,000h life. IEC 60947-5-1, CE, RoHS compliant.
